name: Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river
name_original: Puente Genil – carrera por el casco urbano y el río Genil
description: This easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) jogging route in Puente Genil offers a pleasant mix of urban views and tranquil riverside scenery along the Geni
region_intro: Jogging routes around Puente Genil offer a diverse landscape for runners, situated in the geographical center of Andalusia. The region is characterized by the Genil River, which provides scenic paths through urban areas and natural meanders. Three small mountain ranges—Sierra del Castillo, Sierra Gorda, and Sierra del Niño—offer varied limestone terrain, with Sierra del Castillo reaching 511 meters at Peñarrubia. Additionally, the Vía Verde del Aceite, an old railway line, presents well-developed paths through olive groves and vineyards. Best jogging routes around Puente Genil The most popular jogging route is Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 29 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Genil River through the town. Another top favourite among local runners is Embalse de Cordobilla loop from Puente Genil, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This route circles the Cordobilla Reservoir, offering views of the natural site. Local runners also love the Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail leading through the Cordoba countryside and past Castillo Anzur, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Jogging around Puente Genil is defined by riverside paths, greenways through olive groves, and trails in small mountain ranges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy urban runs to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Puente Genil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 220 runners have used komoot to explore Puente Genil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Puente Genil?
A: There are over 20 diverse running routes available in Puente Genil, catering to various fitness levels. These include easy urban runs, moderate trails through olive groves, and more challenging ascents in the small mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Puente Genil?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001013710/puente-genil-run-through-the-urban-area-and-the-genil-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 2.7-mile (4.4 km) trail is mostly flat and follows the scenic Genil River through the town, making it perfect for beginners or a relaxed jog.

Q: What are some scenic running trails with viewpoints in the area?
A: For scenic views, consider routes that pass by &lt;strong&gt;Castillo Anzur&lt;/strong&gt;. The ruins of this castle offer panoramic vistas of the Cordoba countryside and the meandering Genil River. You can also find beautiful views along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt; near the Malpasillo Reservoir, or from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt; highlight.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options or greenways in Puente Genil?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite (Olive Oil Greenway)&lt;/strong&gt; is a fantastic option for long-distance running. This 128 km greenway, following an old railway line, connects Puente Genil to Jaén, offering well-developed paths through beautiful hilly landscapes filled with olive trees and vineyards. A segment of this is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1548356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jauja – Subbética Greenway Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is also excellent for longer runs.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes around Puente Genil?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For a moderate run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huertos Sociales loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.3-mile (10.1 km) option. For a more challenging and longer circular route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; covers about 8.6 miles (13.9 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of natural features will I encounter on running trails in Puente Genil?
A: Jogging routes in Puente Genil offer a rich variety of natural features. You'll find paths alongside the &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;, through extensive &lt;strong&gt;olive groves and vineyards&lt;/strong&gt;, and within the small mountain ranges like &lt;strong&gt;Sierra del Castillo&lt;/strong&gt;, which features dense scrub vegetation. The region also boasts significant natural protected areas such as the &lt;strong&gt;Cordobilla Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Laguna de Tíscar Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt;, offering serene natural settings.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Yes, many routes blend natural beauty with historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads past the historic &lt;strong&gt;Castillo Anzur&lt;/strong&gt;. While not directly on a featured running route, the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Fuente Álamo Roman Villa&lt;/strong&gt; with its mosaics and the iconic &lt;strong&gt;Stone Bridge&lt;/strong&gt; in town are also notable points of interest in the region.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Puente Genil?
A: The running routes in Puente Genil are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 220 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from riverside paths to greenways and mountain trails, and the opportunities to immerse oneself in nature and local history.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for a 10k distance?
A: Yes, several routes are ideal for a 10k run or can be easily extended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20119208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huertos Sociales loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 6.3 miles (10.1 km), making it a perfect 10k option. For a longer challenge, the Vía Verde del Aceite offers extensive sections that can be tailored to any desired distance beyond 10k.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Puente Genil?
A: Puente Genil, with its mild Andalusian climate, is generally suitable for jogging year-round. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Even in summer, early mornings or late evenings provide cooler conditions for enjoyable runs, especially along shaded riverside paths or the Vía Verde.

Q: Are there any running trails near water bodies or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31478286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cordobilla loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.7-mile (15.7 km) path that circles the &lt;strong&gt;Cordobilla Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, offering beautiful views of this natural site. Additionally, many routes follow the &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;, providing scenic riverside running experiences.
tips: Cordoba municipality already very close to Seville. It is famous, among other things, for its quinces, grown in the extensive orchards on one side of the Genil River. On the other shore, olive trees and vines predominate, typical of the Cordoba countryside. Formerly the two shores were separate municipalities, one in Seville, Miragenil, and another in Córdoba called Puente de Don Gonzalo, but they were finally unified under a single Cordovan municipality in 1834, although Miragenil hardly grew.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Puente Genil urban and river route?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 4.37 km long and generally takes around 28 to 30 minutes to complete, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: Is this jogging route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is! The route is rated as easy with minimal elevation gain (about 60 meters up and down), making it suitable for individuals of any fitness level, including beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Puente Genil urban and river run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly characterized by paved surfaces, offering a comfortable run. While primarily urban, sections along the Genil River may include some easily accessible paths and potentially dirt tracks, but it remains relatively flat throughout.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Genil River run?
faqs.answer: You can expect a pleasant blend of urban views as you pass through Puente Genil's town area, transitioning into more natural riverside landscapes along the Genil River. The route offers views of the river and surrounding nature, combining the town's character with tranquil riverside environments.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or sights along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers a combination of urban and natural sights. A significant landmark you'll encounter is the historic Stone Bridge (Puente de Piedra), which connects the two banks of the river. You'll also pass by the Church of the Immaculate Conception (Puente Genil).

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other paths in Puente Genil?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other local paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino Viejo de la Cuesta del Molino and the Paseo Ayudante de Ingenieros Ricardo Moreno Ortega for portions of your run.

faqs.question: Is the Puente Genil urban and river jogging route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific local regulations for dogs in urban areas and along the riverbanks may apply. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain a pleasant environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any permits or have to pay entrance fees to enjoy the Puente Genil urban and river jogging route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Puente Genil urban and river route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round. However, for the most comfortable experience, consider jogging during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite warm in Puente Genil, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable then.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Puente Genil urban and river jogging route?
faqs.answer: As the route runs through the urban area of Puente Genil, you can typically find street parking in various locations near the river or town center. Look for available public parking spaces within the town to easily access different starting points along the route.

faqs.question: Are there places to get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, since the route passes through the urban area of Puente Genil, you'll find various cafes, bars, and shops where you can purchase refreshments, especially in the town center and along the main streets adjacent to the river.

waypoints.name: Church of the Immaculate Conception (Puente Genil)
